---
title: "Chapter7"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-19"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r}
source('Brownian_motion.R',keep.source = TRUE)
source('EM.R',keep.source = TRUE)
source('plots.R',keep.source = TRUE)

########################################################
# Step 1: Definer 0=t_0<t_1<...<t_N=T og drift- og noise-funktioner
########################################################
t_0 <- 0
N <- 1000000
h <- 0.0001
T <- N*h
times <- seq(t_0,N*h,length.out=N+1)
# noisy_times <- sort(times + rnorm(length(times), mean = 0, sd = 0.1))

# drift term
F <- function(x,theta = c(0.5, -0.05)){
  eps<-theta[1]; y<-theta[2]
  (x[1]-x[1]^3-y)/eps
}


# additive noise term
G <- function(x, Sigma = c(0.6)) Sigma

########################################################
# Step 3: Euler-Maruyama simulation af SDE løsningen med plots. M_reps repetitions.
########################################################

x0 <- c(0)
n_paths <- 10
paths <- EM_sim_fast(F,G,x0,times,n_paths,progress = TRUE)

# Plot første repetition
plot_variable_vs_time(paths[[3]],times,to_time = T)
```


```{r}

# Definer eventuelt sub paths
ratio <- 2000
sub_h <- h*ratio
sub_times <- seq(t_0,N*h,length.out=N/ratio+1)
idx <- seq(1,N+1,by=ratio)
sub_paths <- lapply(paths, function(path) {
  df <- as.data.frame(path)
  # ensure idx are in bounds
  valid_idx <- idx[idx <= nrow(df)]
  df_sub <- df[valid_idx, , drop = FALSE]
  # rename single column to "X" if there is one column; otherwise you might want to keep names
  if (ncol(df_sub) == 1) {
    names(df_sub) <- "X"
  }
  df_sub
})
plot_variable_vs_time(sub_paths[[1]],sub_times,T)



Rcpp::sourceCpp("DoubleWell.cpp")

centers <- list("fix","unbiased")  # single string per center
center_names <- c('Standard fixed point linearization',"Unbiased estimator")

# True parameter vector (for overlay)
true_params <- c(0.5, -0.05, 0.6)

# Starting parameter vector for optimization
start_params <- c(0.2, 0.1, 0.1)

# Labels for parameters
param_labels <- c("epsilon", "y", "sigma")

# ---------------------- helper: single-center fitting -----------------------
fit_for_center <- function(center_vec, show_progress = FALSE) {
  n_paths <- length(paths)
  preds <- vector("list", n_paths)
  
  for (i in seq_along(sub_paths)) {
    if (show_progress) message("  fitting path ", i, " / ", n_paths)
    this_path <- sub_paths[[i]]
    par0 <- start_params
    
    # Objective: MINIMIZE negative log-likelihood
    obj <- function(par, path, times) {
      theta_drift     <- par[1:2]
      theta_diffusion <- par[3]
      
      ll <- tryCatch(
        log_lik_path_rcpp(as.matrix(path), times, theta_drift, theta_diffusion,
                          method = center_vec),  # already a string
        error = function(e) NA_real_
      )
      
      if (!is.numeric(ll) || !is.finite(ll)) {
        return(1e20)
      }
      
      return(ll)  # minimize negative log-likelihood
    }
    
    # Optimize
    fit <- optim(par    = par0,
                 fn     = obj,
                 path   = this_path,
                 times  = sub_times,
                 method = "Nelder-Mead",
                 control = list(maxit = 5000, trace = 0)
    )
    
    preds[[i]] <- fit$par
  }
  
  # Convert results to data.frame
  preds_mat <- do.call(rbind, preds)
  colnames(preds_mat) <- param_labels
  out_df <- as.data.frame(preds_mat)
  return(out_df)
}

# ---------------------- run fits for all centers -----------------------
all_results_list <- vector("list", length(centers))

for (i in seq_along(centers)) {
  cname <- center_names[i]
  cent  <- centers[[i]]
  message("Running fits for center: ", cname)
  
  res_df <- fit_for_center(cent, show_progress = TRUE)
  
  # add run index and center label
  res_df <- res_df %>% 
    mutate(run = seq_len(nrow(res_df)), 
           center = cname)
  
  all_results_list[[i]] <- res_df
}

# Combine results and remove rows where all parameters are NA
all_results <- bind_rows(all_results_list)
all_results <- all_results %>% 
  filter(!apply(select(., all_of(param_labels)), 1, function(r) all(is.na(r))))

# ---------------------- format for plotting -----------------------
est_df <- all_results %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(param_labels),
               names_to = "param",
               values_to = "estimate") %>%
  mutate(param = factor(param, levels = param_labels),
         center = factor(center, levels = center_names))

# expand true values to each center so geom_point can map to center
true_df <- tibble(param = param_labels, true_value = true_params)
true_df_expanded <- expand_grid(param = param_labels, center = center_names) %>%
  left_join(true_df, by = "param") %>%
  mutate(center = factor(center, levels = center_names))

# ---------------------- plotting -----------------------
p <- ggplot(est_df, aes(x = center, y = estimate, fill = center)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.1, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_point(data = true_df_expanded,
             aes(x = center, y = true_value),
             color = "gold", size = 1, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ param, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3, labeller = label_parsed) +
  labs(x = "Method", y = "Estimate") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1)) +
  geom_hline(data = true_df_expanded,
             aes(yintercept = true_value),
             color = "yellow",
             linewidth = 0.6,
             linetype = "dashed",
             inherit.aes = FALSE)

# compute per-facet limits and add invisible points to fix y-scale
y_limits <- est_df %>%
  group_by(param) %>%
  summarize(min_y = min(estimate), max_y = max(estimate), .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(true_df, by = "param") %>%
  mutate(range = max_y - min_y,
         lower = pmin(min_y, true_value - 0.2*range),
         upper = pmax(max_y, true_value + 0.2*range))

y_limits_long <- y_limits %>%
  select(param, lower, upper) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(lower, upper), values_to = "y") %>%
  select(param, y)

p <- p + geom_blank(data = y_limits_long, aes(y = y), inherit.aes = FALSE)

# show plot
print(p)
```
